questions.question: In a stem-and-leaf plot, what does the 'stem' represent?

options.B: The first digit of a number
options.C: The last digit of a number

questions.question: What is the purpose of a stem-and-leaf plot?

options.A: To calculate the mean of a dataset
options.B: To visualize the distribution of a dataset
options.C: To find the median of a dataset
options.D: To determine the range of a dataset
options.E: To identify outliers in a dataset

questions.question: The ages of 10 ancient Egyptian pharaohs are: $23, 25, 30, 32, 35, 40, 42, 45, 50, 55$. What is the stem in the stem-and-leaf plot?
The heights of 8 ancient Greek ruins are: $15, 18, 20, 22, 25, 28, 30, 32$. How many leaves are in the stem-and-leaf plot for this data?
The scores of 9 students on a history test are: $80, 85, 90, 92, 95, 98, 80, 82, 88$. What is the most frequent score in the stem-and-leaf plot?

A stem-and-leaf plot shows the distribution of the number of $\text{artifacts}$ found at an archaeological site. If the stem is $1$ and the leaves are $2, 4, 6, 8$, what are the actual values of the number of artifacts?
In a stem-and-leaf plot, what does a stem of $4$ and leaves of $1, 3, 5$ represent?
A stem-and-leaf plot has a stem of $2$ and leaves of $0, 2, 4, 6, 8$. What is the range of the data?

Create a stem-and-leaf plot for the following ages of 12 ancient Roman emperors: $25, 30, 31, 32, 35, 40, 42, 45, 50, 52, 55, 58$. Identify the median and mode of the data. Provide your answers in the space below.

The following is a stem-and-leaf plot of the lengths of 10 ancient stone bridges: $\text{Stem} \quad \text{Leaves} \quad \quad  3 \quad  1, 4, 5, 7 \quad \quad  4 \quad  0, 2, 3, 9 \quad \quad  5 \quad  1, 6, 8$. Interpret the distribution of the data and provide at least two observations about the data in the space below.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of accurate stem-and-leaf plot construction.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use real-world examples to practice interpreting stem-and-leaf plots.
Tip 3: Remind students to label their axes and provide a key when creating stem-and-leaf plots.
